description:
A Solar Energy Systems Engineer is sizing a 50 MW utility-scale tracking PV system near Phoenix, AZ, where land costs $60,000/acre. Use PySAM to determine which Ground Coverage Ratio (GCR) among 0.3, 0.4, 0.5, 0.6, and 0.7 minimizes the Levelized Cost of Energy (LCOE) by balancing inter-row shading losses against land costs.

System Configuration:
- Weather File: /home/ga/SAM_Weather_Data/phoenix_az_tmy.csv
- System Capacity: 50,000 kW DC
- Module Type: 0 (Standard)
- Array Type: 2 (1-Axis Tracking)
- DC-to-AC Ratio: 1.2
- Inverter Efficiency: 96.0%
- System Losses: 14.08%
- Tilt: 0
- Azimuth: 180

LCOE Calculation Parameters:
- Module Area: 333,333.33 m²
- Land Area: Module Area / GCR (1 acre = 4046.86 m²)
- Total Capital Cost: $50,000,000 (Base) + Land Cost ($60,000/acre)
- Annualized Cost: (Total Capital Cost * 0.08) + $750,000 (O&M)
- LCOE (cents/kWh): (Annualized Cost / annual_energy) * 100

Export the results to /home/ga/Documents/SAM_Projects/gcr_optimization.json. The JSON must include the project location ("Phoenix, AZ"), system capacity, the optimal GCR, and a results array containing the GCR, annual energy (kWh), land area (acres), and LCOE (cents/kWh) for each of the five tested GCR values.
